code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Product failure within 6 months with safety hazards
    - Multiple defect reports across purchases
    - Explicit "return/don't buy" recommendations
    - Updates overriding initial positive experiences

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failures despite good features
    - Cost-benefit analysis showing poor value
    - Comparative statements highlighting inferiority
    - Regret language ("wish I bought...")

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Context-dependent praise ("good for X but not Y")
    - Partial functionality requiring workarounds
    - Balanced material quality vs functionality
    - Temporal satisfaction ("worked initially but...")

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional criticism with explicit star deduction
    - Time-tested performance with minor quirks
    - Price-performance ratio achieving expectations
    - Comparative improvement over previous solutions

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Multi-year performance validation
    - Transformative impact statements
    - Feature diversity exceeding requirements
    - Spontaneous recommendation language

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Non-functional material properties ≠ defects
    - Storage capacity limitations ≠ poor quality
    - Temporary issues with permanent resolutions
    - Specialized excellence for niche use cases
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: UPDATE: Worked 3 years then failed. New version broke in 2 months.
<Reasoning>Systemic quality issues across purchases.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: Silent lid mechanism sticks occasionally. Still my favorite trash can.
<Reasoning>Minor functional flaw with sustained recommendation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Perfect for apartment living but wouldn't trust in commercial kitchen.
<Reasoning>Context-dependent praise with limitation disclosure.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: These organizers tripled my storage space. Worth every penny!
<Reasoning>Transformative impact with financial endorsement.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Cotton wrinkles but absorbs better than more expensive brands.
<Reasoning>Core functionality overcomes inherent material property.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Does the job adequately. Handle feels cheap but holds items.
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy with material criticism.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Loved it until motors failed after 90 days. False durability claims.
<Reasoning>Core failure overriding initial satisfaction.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Great for seasonal storage but wouldn't survive frequent moves.
<Reasoning>Specialized suitability with durability limitation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Transformed my small kitchen layout. Five years and still perfect!
<Reasoning>Long-term validation of transformative benefits.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>
</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (45 total misclassified):
  gold 5 -> 4: 20
  gold 4 -> 5: 10
  gold 4 -> 3: 5
  gold 5 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 2: 3

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "These skull and bones are plenty big to be seen from a distance. You get two in the package and they measure 21"x18.5". You could use them in your home or office. Even though these are the pirate skull and bones, they are smiling and don't look scary..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "Not much to say. They work!"
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [5, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "Very nice set of skillets - and what a lovely color.  Quite a bargain, and I was glad to find it as I provide my husband his "own" skillets.  He tends to be a "high heat" cook, and so I don't let him use my expensive skillets.  Was nice to find Farbe..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 45 errors, 14 borderline out of 80 examples."}
